Tonight's guest is Daisy from the UK.
Daisy, welcome to the show.
Hi, Vic, and thank you for getting in touch and letting me do this.
An amazing experience, and hopefully I will learn from it as well.
I hope you do. And thanks for reaching out to share your experiences with me. I definitely appreciate it. Daisy, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
Okay, so I'm obviously in the UK. I live on a very old farm. The farm is in the Doomsday
book, surrounded by ancient woodlands, fields, rivers, brook streams, lots and lots of underground
Springs. Our farm is way off the road down a long track in anybody that knows the UK,
the Surrey Sussex border. It's an absolutely stunning area. It's very remote, much more
remote than people would think for the UK.
Um, I, I literally, I was born here on the farm.
I was born in the sitting room beside the front room by beside the fire.
And I always grew up and mum completely understand how I, how I feel about this.
I used to say that this land feels like the very womb that I came from because that's
how much it means to me.
I absolutely love it.
It feels like my soul just comes back to be here time and time again.
It's an incredible place.
There were six of us kids and we just grew up playing on the farm, playing hide and seek.
Um, playing up in the woods, we used to grab an old sack and take our toys up in
the sack to the woods and find old trees that were hollow and, and build camps and,
and just having a wonderful time as, as kids should grow up.
And, um, we would at night times, we wouldn't have electric.
We would play, if anybody remembers the game, Hunt the Thimble, where we would all play as one of us would hide a thimble in the house.
And we would just have wonderful times, wonderful memories of growing up here, isolated on this farm.
It was just an incredible experience.
I feel very blessed to have had.
There's old buildings outside, really, really ancient buildings.
We've all grown up, myself and my brothers and sisters, we all grew up seeing spirits around the farm, in the house.

and it's a lovely childhood, really I would say an old-fashioned childhood.
You know, we were just left to do our own thing.
We would go up the woods early in the morning and not get back till dark
and mum wouldn't have to worry about it.
Everything was absolutely fine.
We all had dogs.
At one point there were six dogs in the house.
We've had ponies.
I now have two owls, one of which I found when he was two weeks old.
He'd plopped out the tree as tawny owls do.
And the cattle had trod on him.
So I hand reared him and he now lives in my bedroom with me
and comes up the woods with me and my dogs and sits on my shoulder.
He can't fly very far.
So I feel completely blessed with
how my life has been and how it is now.
I've just turned 60. I never got married. I didn't want kids. I love kids, but I didn't want kids. I just love the farm so much. I stayed here with my mum, looking after her and the farm.

just soaking up this atmosphere.
I think places like this are becoming rarer and rarer,
which is incredibly sad,
because as much as I love being here on my own,
if people were able to be in places like this
this and just sit and soak in the atmosphere, soak in the surroundings.

But here goes Um so around about 20 years ago I um was walking down an old cart track from the farm
I had two dogs with me. I think it was a Westian, a big old cross-fed dog, a big old black shaggy dog.
Very, very happy. So these two dogs were trotting ahead of me, sniffing out rabbits and just having a wonderful time as dogs do.
and I remember that I was looking down at the ground as I was going down this track
thinking about a problem as we a lot of us do and I try not to now
and the first I knew that something was going on was these two dogs that at this point had
been trotting long happily in front of me came flying back to me and stuck in behind my legs
they were leaning against me and as they did that I looked up now ahead of me was a bend
you couldn't see around until you were on it because of the trees either side
coming around this bend and slightly downhill was this creature
um he like i said he was coming down a slight hill so at that point his bum was higher than his front
end and um he was weird it was so weird he was doing exactly the same as me he was looking down
at the ground and in my head it was just like he was doing the same as me looking down at the ground
thinking about problems or something and I must have gasped and he looked up at me
and at the time I remember thinking it was almost like he was young and he was lost and he wasn't
expecting to see me there and he was just as surprised to see me as I was him but I now believe
that that wasn't the case and I think he I think he showed himself to me on purpose anyway
he was at that point he was literally one color all over which was like I describe as and galaxies
sort of chocolate brown he he was at that point as he walked towards me he was walking like a dog

and he didn't have what I hear of in America.

and I thought at that point that he had gold eyes.

I didn't see any sexual parts, but I just seemed to feel that he was male and young.
So as I said, he saw me, he stood up, he span around really, really quickly

he disappeared in front of me.
And so he just vanished.

That's what he did.
And he was gone.
I stood there and there was fear, but there was also the feeling of, you know, I've grown

terrified, but I was scared, but I wasn't terrified.

and I never, never, ever forgot about him.

oh my God, it's beautiful outside.

watch the stars. I just adore being outside on my own, just me and a dog.
